title: Unable to create anonymous volumes with `Mount=` in Quadlet files
body:
### Issue Description

The `Mount=` option in Quadlet files does not support anonymous volumes.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Create a file at `$HOME/.config/containers/systemd/busybox.container` with the following content 
2. Run `/usr/lib/systemd/system-generators/podman-system-generator -dryrun -user`

```
[Container]
Image = docker.io/busybox
Mount = type=volume,dst=/vol
```

### Describe the results you received

`/usr/lib/systemd/system-generators/podman-system-generator -dryrun -user` shows the following error.

```
quadlet-generator[2004095]: Loading source unit file /home/wafrelka/.config/containers/systemd/busybox.container
quadlet-generator[2004095]: converting "busybox.container": source cannot be empty
quadlet-generator[2004095]: processing encountered some errors
```

### Describe the results you expected

`podman-system-generator` runs without errors.

### podman info output

```yaml
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.8.1
API Version:  5.8.1
Go Version:   go1.26.1-X:nodwarf5
Git Commit:   c6077f645788743258a1a749f8005b4fb3cbe533
Built:        Fri Apr  3 18:21:44 2026
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

`podman run` supports anonymous volumes. (ref: #13757)
`podman run --rm -it --mount type=volume,dst=/vol docker.io/busybox` runs successfully.

https://docs.podman.io/en/stable/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html#mount says `Mount=` "is equivalent to the Podman `--mount` option", so I think this is a bug in the quadlet implementation.

comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this. As you pointed out, anonymous volumes do work for mount arguments on the command line. 

However, according to the man page `source` is mandatory for `artifact`, `bind`, `glob`, `image` and `volume`: 
https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-run.1.html#mount-type-type-type-specific-option 

So, I'm not sure how to address this issue. I doubt Podman will be changed to block it now (I don't see a good reason to do that). But, should Quadlet support something that contradicts the man page? Having said that, we've decided in the past that Quadlet should not validate the input and leave it to Podman. The reason it is done here is because `source` may be changed when pointing to another Quadlet file.

So, I think these are our options:

1. Adhere to the man page and not change Quadlet
2. Support empty source only for `type=volume`
3. Not enforce anything in Quadlet and let Podman fail if it does

@Luap99 @mheon @Honny1 WDYT?
Empty source for `type=volume` sounds like it should be allowed. I lean towards this being a bug in the manpages that we should address (and also fix in Quadlet) - so option 2.
It looks like the documentation was forgotten in this PR: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13757, making this a documentation bug. I am leaning towards option 2.
